### Applications to vary, extend or discharge an order E+W


11.10. Rules 11.7 and 11.9 apply to applications to vary, extend or discharge a F39 ... protection order.


Textual Amendments


F39 Words in rule 11.10 omitted (17.7.2015) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2015 (S.I. 2015/1420) , rules 1(2) , 12


### Orders containing provisions to which a power of arrest is attached E+W


F40 11.11. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .


Textual Amendments


F40 Rule 11.11 omitted (1.7.2015) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2015 (S.I. 2015/913) , rules 1 , 10 (with rule 14 )


### [ F41 Notifying the police E+W


11.12. A practice direction may make provision for notifying the police when a protection order is made, varied, extended or discharged. ]


Textual Amendments


F41 Rule 11.12 substituted (1.10.2022) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2022 (S.I. 2022/821) , rules 1(3) , 4


### Application for issue of warrant for arrest E+W


11.13. —(1) An application under section 63J(2) of the 1996 Act [ F42 or under paragraph 7(1) of Part 1 of Schedule 2 to the 2003 Act ] M3 for the issue of a warrant for the arrest of a person must be supported by a sworn statement.


(2) An application for the issue of a warrant for arrest made by a person who is neither the person who is the subject of the proceedings nor (if different) the person who applied for the order, shall be treated, in the first instance, as an application for permission to apply for the warrant to be issued, and the court shall either—


(3) The court officer must inform the following persons of the court's action under paragraph (2)—


Textual Amendments

metadata.title: Part 11 — Applications under Part 4A of the Family Law Act 1996 or Part 1 of Schedule 2 to the Female Genital Mutilation Act 2003
